name: British Antarctic Territory

colors.symbolism: the field — the snow and ice of Antarctica
the cross of St George and the lion of the territorial arms

description: A British White Ensign — a white field with the cross of St George and the Union Flag in the canton — defaced in the fly with the coat of arms of the British Antarctic Territory.
history: Flag of the British Antarctic Territory, a British Overseas Territory comprising part of Antarctica south of 60°S between longitudes 20°W and 80°W. The flag was granted by Queen Elizabeth II in 1963. The current flag (with refinements) has been in use since 1998.

features.role: Coat of arms of the British Antarctic Territory
